{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"Break the import from `src/domain/billing.ts` to `src/presentation/invoice-view.ts`.

Why:

- It is part of the 3-file cycle:
  `billing-flow.ts -> billing.ts -> invoice-view.ts -> billing-flow.ts`
- It is also the explicit architecture violation:
  `domain` imports `presentation`, but the rule says `domain cannotImport presentation`.
- The domain layer should not know about invoice rendering/view code. That dependency points in the wrong direction and is the most likely runtime-cycle trigger.

Best fix: move whatever `billing.ts` needs from `invoice-view.ts` into a neutral module, or invert the dependency.

For example:

- If `billing.ts` only needs a type from `invoice-view.ts`, change it to `import type`, assuming no runtime value is needed.
- If it needs formatting/rendering behavior, extract a domain-owned interface or plain shared helper, e.g. `src/domain/invoice-types.ts` or `src/shared/invoice-format.ts`, then have both `billing.ts` and `invoice-view.ts` import that.
- Keep `invoice-view.ts` depending on domain data, not the other way around.

I would not start by breaking the `billing-flow.ts` imports, because `application -> domain` and `presentation -> application` may be intentional orchestration paths. The clearly wrong edge is `domain -> presentation`, and removing it fixes both the cycle pressure and the layer violation."}}
